original: That everyone’s too busy these days is a cliché.
translation_literal: 如今每个人都太忙这个说法是陈词滥调。

chunks.english: That everyone’s too busy these days

chunks.relation: 作主句 is 的表语

original: But one specific complaint is made especially mournfully: There’s never any time to read.
translation_literal: 但是有一种具体抱怨被格外悲伤地提出：从来没有任何时间阅读。

chunks.english: There’s never any time to read
chunks.chinese: 从来没有任何时间阅读
chunks.relation: 冒号后解释 complaint 的内容
解释内容中的 there be 结构

sentence_patterns.pattern_form: 抽象名词 + 冒号解释
sentence_patterns.explanation: 冒号后的 There’s... 解释 complaint 的具体内容。

original: What makes the problem thornier is that the usual time-management techniques don’t seem sufficient.
translation_literal: 使这个问题更加棘手的是，通常的时间管理技巧似乎不够。

chunks.chinese: 使这个问题更加棘手的东西

chunks.relation: 补充说明 problem 的状态

chunks.english: that the usual time-management techniques don’t seem sufficient
chunks.chinese: 通常的时间管理技巧似乎不够

sentence_patterns.pattern_name: 分裂结构 / 间隔结构
sentence_patterns.pattern_form: What 从句 + is + that 从句
sentence_patterns.explanation: 句子两端都是从句，What 从句作主语，that 从句作表语，容易误判主干。

original: The web’s full of articles offering tips on making time to read: “Give up TV” or “Carry a book with you at all times”.
translation_literal: 网络上满是提供挤出阅读时间建议的文章：“放弃电视”或者“随时带一本书”。

chunks.english: full of articles offering tips on making time to read
chunks.chinese: 满是提供挤出阅读时间建议的文章

chunks.english: offering tips on making time to read
chunks.chinese: 提供挤出阅读时间建议的

chunks.english: “Give up TV” or “Carry a book with you at all times”
chunks.chinese: “放弃电视”或者“随时带一本书”
chunks.relation: 冒号后举例说明 tips 的内容

sentence_patterns.explanation: 冒号后两个引号内容是 tips 的例子，不是新的主干。

original: But in my experience, using such methods to free up the odd 30 minutes doesn’t work.
translation_literal: 但是根据我的经验，使用这些方法腾出零散的 30 分钟并不起作用。

chunks.english: using such methods to free up the odd 30 minutes
chunks.chinese: 使用这些方法腾出零散的 30 分钟

chunks.english: to free up the odd 30 minutes

original: Sit down to read and the flywheel of work-related thoughts keeps spinning — or else you’re so exhausted that a challenging book’s the last thing you need.
translation_literal: 坐下来阅读时，与工作相关的思绪飞轮仍在旋转，或者你太疲惫，以至于一本有挑战性的书是你最不需要的东西。

chunks.relation: 第一并列分句的祈使谓语
说明 Sit down 的目的

chunks.english: the flywheel of work-related thoughts keeps spinning
chunks.chinese: 与工作相关的思绪飞轮仍在旋转
chunks.relation: 第二分句，说明坐下后仍然无法进入阅读

chunks.chinese: 与工作相关的思绪飞轮

chunks.english: or else you’re so exhausted that a challenging book’s the last thing you need
chunks.chinese: 或者你太疲惫，以至于一本有挑战性的书是你最不需要的东西
chunks.relation: 第三分句，说明另一种无法阅读的情况

chunks.english: that a challenging book’s the last thing you need
chunks.chinese: 以至于一本有挑战性的书是你最不需要的东西

chunks.relation: that 结果从句内部主语
that 结果从句内部谓语

sentence_patterns.pattern_form: 祈使句 + and + 分句 + or else + 分句
sentence_patterns.explanation: 句子用多个并列分句展示两种阅读失败的情形。

original: The modern mind, Tim Parks, a novelist and critic, writes, “is overwhelmingly inclined toward communication…It is not simply that one is interrupted; it is that one is actually inclined to interruption”.
translation_literal: 小说家兼评论家蒂姆·帕克斯写道，现代心智“压倒性地倾向于交流……并不只是一个人被打断；而是一个人实际上倾向于被打断”。

chunks.english: Tim Parks, a novelist and critic, writes
chunks.chinese: 小说家兼评论家蒂姆·帕克斯写道

chunks.relation: 解释 Tim Parks 的身份
作 inclined toward 的宾语

chunks.english: It is not simply that one is interrupted
chunks.chinese: 并不只是一个人被打断
chunks.relation: 引语中的第一解释分句
第一解释分句的形式主语

chunks.english: it is that one is actually inclined to interruption
chunks.chinese: 而是一个人实际上倾向于被打断
chunks.relation: 引语中的第二解释分句
It is not simply... 分句内部谓语
第二个 it is... 分句内部形式主语
第二个 it is... 分句内部谓语

chunks.english: that one is actually inclined to interruption
chunks.chinese: 一个人实际上倾向于被打断
chunks.relation: 真正主语，对应第二个 it

sentence_patterns.pattern_form: 主语 + 发言人插入 + 谓语
sentence_patterns.explanation: Tim Parks...writes 插入主语和谓语之间，主干是 The modern mind is inclined...。
引语中两个 it is 结构都需要把 that 从句还原为真正主语/说明内容。

original: Deep reading requires not just time, but a special kind of time which can’t be obtained merely by becoming more efficient.
translation_literal: 深度阅读需要的不只是时间，而是一种特殊的时间，这种时间不能仅仅通过变得更高效来获得。

chunks.english: not just time, but a special kind of time
chunks.chinese: 不只是时间，而是一种特殊的时间

chunks.english: which can’t be obtained merely by becoming more efficient
chunks.chinese: 这种时间不能仅仅通过变得更高效来获得
chunks.relation: 修饰 a special kind of time

sentence_patterns.explanation: 宾语不是单纯的 time，而是 a special kind of time，后面 which 从句修饰它。

original: In fact, “becoming more efficient” is part of the problem.
translation_literal: 事实上，“变得更高效”是问题的一部分。

original: Thinking of time as a resource to be maximized means you approach it instrumentally, judging any given moment as well spent only in so far as it advances progress toward some goal.
translation_literal: 把时间看作一种要被最大化利用的资源意味着你会工具性地对待它，只有在某个时刻推动了朝向某个目标的进展时，才判断它被很好地花费了。

chunks.english: Thinking of time as a resource to be maximized
chunks.chinese: 把时间看作一种要被最大化利用的资源

chunks.english: you approach it instrumentally, judging any given moment as well spent only in so far as it advances progress toward some goal
chunks.chinese: 你会工具性地对待它，只有在某个时刻推动了朝向某个目标的进展时，才判断它被很好地花费了

chunks.relation: 作 approach 的宾语，指 time

chunks.english: judging any given moment as well spent
chunks.chinese: 判断任何给定时刻被很好地花费
chunks.relation: 补充说明 approach 的具体方式

chunks.english: only in so far as it advances progress toward some goal
chunks.chinese: 只有在它推动朝某个目标的进展时
chunks.relation: 说明 judging 的条件范围
as 从句内部主语，指 any given moment

sentence_patterns.pattern_name: 分裂结构 / 间隔结构
sentence_patterns.pattern_form: 动名词主语 + means + 宾语从句 + 非谓语
sentence_patterns.explanation: 主语很长，means 后宾语从句又带 judging 和 as 从句，需要分层读。

original: Immersive reading, by contrast, depends on being willing to risk inefficiency, goallessness, even time-wasting.
translation_literal: 相比之下，沉浸式阅读取决于愿意冒低效、无目标、甚至浪费时间的风险。

chunks.relation: 提示与前一句形成对比

chunks.english: being willing to risk inefficiency, goallessness, even time-wasting
chunks.chinese: 愿意冒低效、无目标、甚至浪费时间的风险
chunks.relation: 作 depends on 的宾语

chunks.chinese: 低效、无目标、甚至浪费时间

sentence_patterns.explanation: by contrast 插入主语和谓语之间，提示语义转折。

original: Try to slot it as a to-do list item and you’ll manage only goal-focused reading — useful, sometimes, but not the most fulfilling kind.
translation_literal: 试图把它塞进待办事项清单，你就只能做到目标导向的阅读，有时有用，但不是最令人满足的那种阅读。

chunks.english: Try to slot it as a to-do list item
chunks.chinese: 试图把它塞进待办事项清单

chunks.relation: 作 slot 的宾语，指阅读

chunks.chinese: 作为待办事项清单项目
你就只能做到目标导向的阅读

chunks.english: useful, sometimes, but not the most fulfilling kind
chunks.chinese: 有时有用，但不是最令人满足的那种

sentence_patterns.pattern_form: 祈使句 + and + 结果分句
sentence_patterns.explanation: Try... and you’ll... 表示如果这样做就会导致后面的结果。
破折号后的 useful... 评价 goal-focused reading。

original: “The future comes at us like empty bottles along an unstoppable and nearly infinite conveyor belt,” writes Gary Eberle in his book Sacred Time, and “we feel a pressure to fill these different-sized bottles (days, hours, minutes) as they pass, for if they get by without being filled, we will have wasted them”.
translation_literal: 加里·埃伯尔在他的《神圣时间》一书中写道：“未来像空瓶子一样沿着一条不可阻挡且近乎无限的传送带向我们而来”，并且“我们感到一种压力，要在这些不同大小的瓶子（日、小时、分钟）经过时填满它们，因为如果它们未经填充就过去了，我们就会浪费它们。”

chunks.english: The future comes at us like empty bottles along an unstoppable and nearly infinite conveyor belt
chunks.chinese: 未来像空瓶子一样沿着不可阻挡且近乎无限的传送带向我们而来

chunks.english: like empty bottles along an unstoppable and nearly infinite conveyor belt
chunks.chinese: 像空瓶子一样沿着不可阻挡且近乎无限的传送带
chunks.relation: 说明 future comes 的方式

chunks.english: writes Gary Eberle in his book Sacred Time
chunks.chinese: 加里·埃伯尔在《神圣时间》一书中写道

chunks.english: we feel a pressure to fill these different-sized bottles (days, hours, minutes) as they pass
chunks.chinese: 我们感到一种压力，要在这些不同大小的瓶子经过时填满它们

chunks.english: a pressure to fill these different-sized bottles
chunks.chinese: 一种填满这些不同大小瓶子的压力

chunks.english: for if they get by without being filled, we will have wasted them
chunks.chinese: 因为如果它们未经填充就过去了，我们就会浪费它们

chunks.english: if they get by without being filled
chunks.chinese: 如果它们未经填充就过去了
chunks.relation: 说明 will have wasted 的条件
原因从句中的结果主句
for 原因从句中结果分句的主语
for 原因从句中结果分句的谓语
作 will have wasted 的宾语

sentence_patterns.pattern_form: 引语 A + writes... + and 引语 B
sentence_patterns.explanation: 两个引语分句并列，中间插入 writes...，第二分句里还有 as 和 if 从句。

sentence_patterns.pattern_form: writes Gary Eberle in his book...
sentence_patterns.explanation: writes... 插在两个引语之间，只说明出处。

original: No mind-set could be worse for losing yourself in a book.
translation_literal: 没有哪种心态对沉浸于一本书更糟糕。

chunks.english: for losing yourself in a book
chunks.chinese: 对于沉浸于一本书来说
chunks.relation: 说明 worse 的适用方面

original: So what does work?
translation_literal: 那么，什么确实有效呢？

sentence_patterns.pattern_form: 疑问词 + 助动词 + 主语 + 谓语
sentence_patterns.explanation: 疑问句中 does 提前，work 是实义动词。

original: Perhaps surprisingly, scheduling regular times for reading.
translation_literal: 也许令人惊讶的是，安排固定的阅读时间。

chunks.relation: 根据上一句 So what does work? 补出的谓语

sentence_patterns.pattern_form: 回答上一句疑问，省略谓语
sentence_patterns.explanation: 本句承接 So what does work?，完整理解为 scheduling regular times for reading works。

original: You’d think this might fuel the efficiency mind-set, but in fact, Eberle notes, such ritualistic behavior helps us “step outside time’s flow” into “soul time”.
translation_literal: 你可能会认为这会助长效率心态，但事实上，埃伯尔指出，这种仪式化行为帮助我们“走出时间之流”，进入“灵魂时间”。

chunks.english: You’d think this might fuel the efficiency mind-set
chunks.chinese: 你可能会认为这会助长效率心态

chunks.english: this might fuel the efficiency mind-set
such ritualistic behavior helps us “step outside time’s flow” into “soul time”
chunks.chinese: 这种仪式化行为帮助我们走出时间之流，进入灵魂时间
chunks.relation: 第二分句，与第一分句转折

chunks.english: step outside time’s flow into soul time
chunks.chinese: 走出时间之流，进入灵魂时间
chunks.relation: 补充说明帮助我们做什么

sentence_patterns.explanation: but 连接预期和实际结论，中间 Eberle notes 是插入语。

original: You could limit distractions by reading only physical books, or on single-purpose e-readers.
translation_literal: 你可以通过只阅读实体书，或者在单一用途的电子阅读器上阅读，来限制干扰。

chunks.english: by reading only physical books, or on single-purpose e-readers
chunks.chinese: 通过只阅读实体书，或者在单一用途的电子阅读器上阅读
实体书 / 单一用途电子阅读器

original: “Carry a book with you at all times” can actually work, too — providing you dip in often enough, so that reading becomes the default state from which you temporarily surface to take care of business, before dropping back down.
translation_literal: “随时带一本书”其实也能起作用，前提是你足够经常地翻读，这样阅读就会成为默认状态，你会从这种状态中暂时浮出来处理事务，然后再沉回去。

chunks.english: “Carry a book with you at all times”
providing you dip in often enough
chunks.chinese: 前提是你足够经常地翻读

chunks.english: so that reading becomes the default state from which you temporarily surface to take care of business, before dropping back down
chunks.chinese: 这样阅读就会成为默认状态，你会从这种状态中暂时浮出来处理事务，然后再沉回去
chunks.relation: 说明满足条件后的结果
so that 从句内部主语
so that 从句内部谓语

chunks.english: from which you temporarily surface to take care of business
chunks.chinese: 你会从中暂时浮出来处理事务的

chunks.relation: from which 从句内部主语
from which 从句内部谓语
说明 surface 后的时间顺序

sentence_patterns.pattern_name: 分裂结构 / 间隔结构
sentence_patterns.pattern_form: 主句 + providing 从句 + so that 从句 + from which 从句
sentence_patterns.explanation: 多个从句连续出现，要先抓住主干 Carry a book... can work。

original: On a really good day, it no longer feels as if you’re “making time to read,” but just reading, and making time for everything else.
translation_literal: 在真正好的日子里，感觉不再像是你在“挤时间阅读”，而只是阅读，并为其他所有事情挤时间。

chunks.english: as if you’re “making time to read,” but just reading, and making time for everything else
chunks.chinese: 好像你不是在挤时间阅读，而只是在阅读，并为其他所有事情挤时间
chunks.relation: 作 feels 的表语内容
as if 从句内部主语
as if 从句内部谓语
与 making time to read 形成转折并列

chunks.chinese: 为其他所有事情挤时间

sentence_patterns.pattern_form: not A, but B, and C
sentence_patterns.explanation: not...but... 后还有 and making...，表示阅读成为默认状态，其他事才需要安排时间。
